hero.title: Spec-driven scheduling for multi-tenant booking
hero.subtitle: Configure resources, slots, and policies once—serve many industries without hard-coding a single workflow.
hero.statusNote: Automation handles the workflow; final confirmation authority is always defined by each merchant’s configuration.
hero.mockDisclaimer: Slots are computed from rules and exceptions—this card is a static illustration only.

features.title: Core capabilities
features.intro: Composable rules, strategies, slot computation, and collaboration—described with shared vocabulary.
features.c1b: Express policies as data and strategies instead of one-off conditionals.
features.c2b: Attach validation and pricing behaviors per resource type.
features.c3b: Compute availability from hours and exceptions rather than pre-materializing every minute.
features.c4b: Clients, merchants, and operators share the same booking lifecycle with clear transitions.

process.title: How it works
process.notice: Actual booking states follow tenant rules and resource policies.
process.s1b: Create your tenant space and invite operators.
process.s2b: Model bookable entities, hours, exceptions, and strategy hooks.
process.s3b: Share storefront or embed flows—clients pick slots that respect your policies.
process.s4b: Monitor utilization and evolve rules without rewriting the core pipeline.

use_cases.title: Resource types you can model
use_cases.intro: Examples use different domains but the same Resource/Slot pipeline.
use_cases.u1b: Recurring cohorts, seat caps, and instructor or room metadata per slot.
use_cases.u2b: Spatial allocation, equipment manifests, and blackout windows.
use_cases.u3b: 1:1 availability, skills metadata, and policy-aware slot offers.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1: Depends on catalog size and rule complexity; start with defaults and iterate.
faq.q2: How is tenant data isolated technically?
faq.a2: Configuration, SEO, and client caches are keyed by tenant + locale + variant. The API rejects cross-tenant fallbacks—content never bleeds across tenants.
faq.a3: This page describes capabilities; commercial terms are agreed per deployment.
faq.q4: Are bookings auto-confirmed by the platform?
faq.a4: No universal guarantee. Confirmation is a state transition governed by each tenant’s rules, strategies, and manual overrides—exactly like production booking engines.

cta.title: Ready to run your booking business on spec?
cta.body: Open the merchant console to configure resources, or browse the client experience.
